hypothesis: If a 3D electron-density encoder is trained contrastively against a SMILES encoder on paired molecules, then its embeddings will become pose-invariant and outperform unimodal and SE(3)-equivariant baselines on unseen-rotation retrieval, structure–property clustering (lower Davies–Bouldin index), and linear-probe property prediction because cross-modal alignment drives the 3D representation to capture pose-agnostic chemical identity.
